🐛 New: dump and debug commands in abapgit-agent

Two new commands to help investigate ABAP runtime issues directly from the terminal (or via AI):

abapgit-agent dump — inspect ST22 short dumps without opening SAP GUI → docs

abapgit-agent debug — set breakpoints, attach, step through code, and inspect variables → docs

Both commands can be called automatically by AI agents — check out the troubleshooting section in abap/CLAUDE.md to see how Claude picks the right tool depending on whether there's a dump or a logic bug to trace.

See it in action: Claude used debug to trace how abapGit decides which files to pull and identified several bugs in the process.

entry.title: Re: MTO and STO (Scope item 1P9)
entry.content:
Good day @Moon3 

Thank you for your question.

You can use MTO and you can use Intercompany Stock Transfer in Public Cloud, but you cannot combine them in the way you described. The point to note is, Scope item 1P9 is a stock transfer process. It stars from a purchase order in the receiving company and drives deliveries and goods movement. It is not a sales order driven MTO process, so an STO does not trigger a Sales Order in standard 1P9.

See Blog: Intercompany Stock Transfer (1P9) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for more insights.

Here are answers to your main question.

Question: Is there any way that I also use Intercompany stock transfer process ( scope item 1P9) with MTO.

Answer: Not as “STO first, then SO, then MRP”. In 1P9, the receiving company creates the intercompany PO and the supplying company performs delivery and billing steps. There is no standard step where the system automatically creates a customer sales order from that STO in classis 1P9.

In Public Cloud, you can achieve MTO + intercompany supply by using a sales order driven intercompany scenario where the Sales Order serves as the trigger and the system creates follow on purchasing or supplying documents accordingly. In other words, the correct direction flow for MTO is as follows:

SO --> triggers supply from affiliate plant --> planning/procurement/production in supplying entity --> delivery --> billing.

If your requirement is specifically for SO to trigger intercompany procurement and production, then you should evaluate Advance Intercompany Sales scope content.

See the following reference for more insights

Exploring the Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP) Scenario

See Blog: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for more details.

Question: But I do not know how STO can trigger SO?

Answer: It does not in classic 1P9. The reason is, 1P9 is designed as an intercompany stock transfer flow and not an intercompany customer order flow.

If you really need an SO to be created in supplying company, that is typically covered by advanced intercompany processes, not classic 1P9.

My recommendation is to use a sales order driven intercompany scenario. If it is MTO with production, evaluated the advanced intercompany sales MTO scope content.

Alternatively, you can keep 1P9 purely for stock transfer with no SO. The design will remain:

  • HQ creates STO
  • Supplying entity delivers
  • Receiving entity receives

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: No receivers were found for segment error When Using...
entry.content:
Good day @R_Pooja 

Thank you for your question.

From your screenshot, the system message typically means it did not find any receiver records that match your Receiver Basis and Receiver Tracing Factor selection for your defined period, so it cannot weight the variable portions.

The Public Cloud offers you the possibility for:

  • Variable Portion plus Receiver Basis equalling Actual Quantities, but it only works if the quantity field group you selected actually has receiver quantity values in the relevant period and the receiver selection matches those postings.
  • If the quantity is posted somewhere else (wrong field group, wrong ledger, wrong period, etc.), the Manage Allocations will return no receivers found. See KBA 3609752 for more insights.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/cb2b3307a05c4d669d04d2bf13c26b38.html?locale=en-US https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/51f37e43ceab4444b752111f7868a482.html?locale=en-US https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3407708

So, what you are seeing is common for Variable Portion with Actual Quantities if the selected field group and selection criteria do not point to quantity postings that are attributed to the receiver object.

Please validate in the segment that the Receiver Basis Type is Actual Quantities and the correct field group is selected, then verify in Manage Journal Entries that the receiver basis GL account line items have both as receiver cost center and a posted quantity in the same period and ledger. If the quantity is posted without the receiver assignment, the allocation will not recognise receivers.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Purpose of Field Group Status “Actual Quantities” in...
entry.content:
Good day @R_Pooja 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, Field Groups in Manage Allocations define which fields and measures the allocation engine reads when it calculates sender values and receiver tracing factors. The “Actual Quantities“ Field Group status indicated that the segment is intended to use posted actual quantity measures as the basis for receiving weighting. Basically saying, which fields and measures the allocation engine is allowed to read for building:

  • Sender values (what you allocate), and
  • Receiver tracing factors (how you split it).

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/4adf8178f6a940579e766311ca91fdf8.html?locale=en-US

 

Regarding how it influences allocations when you use Variable Portion à Actual Quantity:

When you set a segment like this:

  • Receiver Rule = Variable Portions
  • Receiver Basis Type = Actual Quantities

The system uses actual quantities to calculate the receiver split:

  1. It reads receiver relevant postings that match your Receiver Basis selection (period, ledger, company code, basis account, etc).
  2. It sums the actual quantities per receiver (for example per cost center) using the chosen Field Group.
  3. It allocates sender amounts proportionally as follow:
  • Receiver share = Receiver quantity divided by Total quantity
  • Allocated amount = Sender amount multiplied by Receiver share.

In summary, when you use Variable Portions with Actual Quantities Receiver Basis Type, the system sums actual quantities per receiver and allocates sender values proportionally to each receiver’s quantity share. If the selected field group or selection criteria do not point to posting where both the receiver object and quantities exist, the allocation cannot determine receiver shares.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/4c8999e494f14169b52ccb1f588c4de2.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/cb2b3307a05c4d669d04d2bf13c26b38.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Claude Code via MCP: Poor man's Joule, or a practical tool?
entry.content:
In my previous blog posts (1) and (2) I described vibe coding in ABAP with Claude Code and OpenAI Codex by means of Node.js-based MCP Servers. I today's blog I'll use an MCP Server built by Alice Vinogradova: https://github.com/oisee/vibing-steampunk?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block.

Alice's solution is different in that:

  1. It installs as a single binary,
  2. it is more clever: It tries to unburden the AI by taking care of certain routine operations (such as locking / unlocking Objects for changes), so that AI can concentrate on the stuff that matters.

Here is Alice's oft-quoted article in which she explains her approach better than I ever could.

Spoiler: I cannot confirm with 100% certainty that Alice's solution improves AI capabilities, but I saw strong empirical evidence to that effect.

As usual, I asked an Agent in Antigravity to do the installation:

Then I used Claude Code CLI to do various changes to an SALV Report I had previously created with Codex (a simple Report with some travel data from a DB Table I created time ago for the RAP100 course). In particular, I asked Claude to:

  • Add Customer Name, Agency Name and Status Description to the Report. For this, Claude had to figure out additional data sources.
  • Add a select "box" column to the ALV.
  • Enable selection on an individual cell level in the ALV.
  • Display Trip Prices > 10.000 of any Currency in red.
  • Add ALV Layout to the Selection Screen. If no Layout selected, then display the results totalled and subtotalled in a certain way.
  • Transform the SELECT Statement in the Report into a CDS View, then use that CDS View.

Here are some conversation examples:

You will notice that Claude went above and beyond by also adding F4 Help to the Layout Field in the Selection Screen.

A keen eye might also notice that I switched to the lesser Sonnet (4.6) model so as not to keep running into rate limits all the time. I didn't observe any degradation of performance, which might at least in part be explained by Alice's very excellent work on reducing the model's mental load by what she calls "composite tools" (again: read the article).

You see that I kept my prompting deliberately vague. "Use the same Package and Transport Request for the CDS View." Like, the same one as the Program uses, or what? None of this threw Claude off.

 

One of the use cases Alice mentions for her MCP Server is data analysis, and I wanted to test it. I asked Claude questions like "How many trips more expensive than 10000 EUR are there?", without specifying any tool or Program or data source to use. He derived these things from the context of our conversation and responded correctly.

Then I asked "How many of these are holidays, how many business trips?" 

See what I did there? Used the word "holidays" when the table entries are described as "Vacation", "Sightseeing in Frankfurt", or "Visiting Pierre".

Claude categorised the trips correctly, even though in the end he used a rather simplistic (though perfectly reasonable) approach: Everything starting with "Business" is a business trip, everything else is holidays.

To make Claude's life more difficult, I changed descriptions of 3 trips from "Business Trip" to: "Client visit", "IT conference" and "Visiting company Branch in Tulsa". This tripped him up - he simply applied the logic that had served him so well previously, and mis-categorised these 3 Trips as leisure. I pointed out the mistake and asked him to look closely at the descriptions. Then he got it right.

So much for some very simple examples of data analysis.

Next I wanted to come back to the RAP / Fiori app topic. I asked Claude to scaffold a RAP Demo using the Northwind Traders Database model originally from Microsoft - the simplified version. He searched the web for info and then created the following artefacts. All by his lonesome, in about 30 minutes.

I have yet to look at them, but I do know one thing for sure: The RAP Application does not work. I suspect the reason might be that Claude forgot to include in the underlying Database Tables the Fields 

  • LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_BY,
  • LOCAL_LAST_CHANGED_AT,
  • LAST_CHANGED_AT, 

which are vital for the locking mechanism in RAP Managed scenarios.

Or it could be something else. In any case, this is a typical bit of knowledge that would go into a Claude Skill. I hear Skills are much improved in their Version 2 which just came out couple of days ago - apparently, they are now testable, and you can pretty much guarantee that they will be invoked.

It seems Skills are misunderstood in the same way as MCPs were a year ago (if you hear someone say that MCP is like the USB-C port but for AI, you are now legally allowed to punch them in the face, did you know that?)  What is especially misunderstood about Skills is their Progressive Disclosure feature - people write 1000-line Skill files and then wonder why their model got dumber. We ABAPers wouldn't do that, because we know about modularisation, amiright? Except those few who still write God Classes with 13000 lines of code. But they are not here - they don't read technology blogs.

So what do you guys think, is Claude / Codex via MCP just a poor man's Joule, or might it aspire to fill the large Joule-shaped hole in our lives to at least some extent?

 

P.S.: No AI was involved in writing the texts of these Blog posts. If it had been, you wouldn't know it, because I would have found the Wikipedia page on AI-sounding writing, fed it to the AI and instructed it to avoid those patterns. But I didn't 😊

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers

entry.title: Re: OBYC is maintained after that Account determination f...
entry.content:
Dear @deepa_khatri,

This behavior is expected in both Public and Private Cloud. SAP determines the G/L account using an exact combination of values such as transaction key, account grouping code, valuation grouping code, valuation class and chart of accounts. For goods movements the account grouping code is used to further split postings, especially for transaction key GBB depending on the movement type and other indicators. Therefore, if the posting is derived with a blank account modifier but configuration is maintained only for VAX or VAY the system will not find a matching entry and an error is expected

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-supply-chain-business-scenarios-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition/setting-up-account-determination_c2813e02-88a0-422f-af4a-28bc70b3179b

https://learning.sap.com/courses/cross-functional-customizing-in-sap-s-4hana-materials-management/subdividing-a-transaction-with-the-account-grouping-code

VAX and VAY are not generic substitutes for a blank modifier. SAP uses them for specific sales-order-related scenarios. VAY when the sales order item carries costs and revenues and VAX when it does not. If your current posting is not one of those scenarios then using a blank modifier can be fully correct

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/5e23dc8fe9be4fd496f8ab556667ea05/1e20ba538c95b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

So, maintaining the account modifier as blank is not wrong by itself. It simply means the posting will use the more general account determination line, instead of being split to a separate G/L account through a dedicated modifier such as VAX or VAY         

Summary:

Keep the blank entry if that is the key your business transaction actually uses and maintain VAX/VAY only as additional entries for the specific sales-order scenarios where SAP derives those modifiers. Do not replace a required blank entry with VAX/VAY

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch | SAP Community Meetup (Cologne #5)
entry.content:
Hallo Zusammen,

nächste Woche starten die DSAG Technologietage und der TechXchange in Hamburg. Direkt danach am Freitag, den 20. März,  findet SAP Stammtisch #5 in Köln statt, falls du noch Energie nach der Woche hast, komm gern vorbei uns lass uns über die Themen der Tage diskutieren. Um 18 Uhr treffen wir uns im CAFE ESPECIAL, direkt beim Bahnhof Messe/Deutz. Wir freuen uns auf dich und falls du zum ersten Mal dabei bist, es wird ein entspannter Abend ohne Zwang und mit offenen Ohren.

Grüße
Orga-Team

###

Hello everyone,

next week, the DSAG Technology Days and TechXchange kick off in Hamburg. Immediately afterward, on Friday 20th March, SAP Community Meetup #5 will take place in Cologne. If you still have some energy after the week, feel free to join us and let's discuss the topics from the event. We'll meet at 6 p.m. at CAFE ESPECIAL, right by the Messe/Deutz train station. We look forward to seeing you, and if this is your first time, it will be a relaxed evening with no pressure and an open mind.

Best regards,

The Organizing Team

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

This CodeJam will take place the day before of the SIT Madrid 2026. For more information, check out https://sitmadrid.com. You can RSVP for the SIT Madrid here.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, April 16, 2026 from 09:30 - 14:00 local time in Madrid, Spain. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: NEORIS Artificial Intelligence Hub, powered by EPAM, Calle de María de Portugal, Madrid, Spain 

  • The event is kindly being hosted by EPAM Neoris
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

📢 Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0

Electronic Batch Records (EBR)

Critical for regulated industries such as life sciences and medical devices, enabling structured operator workflows and traceability.

The Electronic Batch Record (EBR) capability enables the creation, review, and approval of document-driven batch records directly within SAP Digital Manufacturing. It supports structured batch documentation with role-based authorization, versioning, and a comprehensive audit trail, enabling compliance-focused production scenarios. This addresses the challenge of managing batch documentation across multiple systems or through manual, paper-based processes.

This way, EBR improves compliance with regulatory requirements such as GMP while reducing manual effort and documentation errors. It also establishes a consistent digital record that increases transparency, traceability, and readiness for audits.

Design Time

Run Time

Harmonized Component Consumption

Bridging discrete and process execution logic under a unified interaction model.

Design Time for the Harmonized Consume Component Dialog

Design Time for Order POD

Run Time for Order POD

Design Time for Work Center POD

Run Time for Work Center POD

Advanced Barcode / Data Scanning

Multi-data barcode capture that feeds automation, labeling, and backend integration flows.

The data scan capability enables barcode-driven execution of production activities directly within the POD by configuring barcode parsing and mapping rules. Scanned barcodes can be interpreted using predefined structures and automatically trigger actions such as assembly, data collection, or SFC start, without additional manual steps. This addresses the inefficiency and error risk of manual data entry and navigation during execution.

From a business standpoint, Data Scan reduces operator interaction time, accelerates production steps, and improves data accuracy on the shop floor. It also provides a scalable foundation for automation by reusing configurations across multiple execution scenarios.

Configuration Time

Using new Configure Data Scanning app.

Design Time

Run Time

These capabilities are built on POD 2.0 and represent the direction of future execution functionality.

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Advanced intercompany stock transfer with valuated stock-in-transit(VSiT) for difference in receipt
entry.content:
Valuated stock-in-transit (VSiT) means that the goods have left the plant of the seller physically, but the ownership (title or risk) of goods has not yet been transferred from the seller to the buyer i.e the seller still holds ownership of goods. Ownership is transferred at some point during the transit generally at the incoterm location or at a location mutually agreed between the seller and buyer.

Advanced intercompany stock transfer uses VSiT to transfer the ownership of goods between delivering company , intermediate company (in case of multistage ) and receiving company.

There was a limitation in confirmation of goods receipt with difference in quantity for EWM managed storage location till S/4HANA2025 release as stated in SAP note 1822497 as in below image. This limitation has been eliminated in 2025 FPS01 release. My present blog is to discuss the new feature in context of advanced intercompany stock transfer(This new feature also works with classic intercompany stock transfer and intracompany stock transfer).

A typical process flow for VSiT with advanced intercompany stock transfer with delivering company 1810 US and receiving company 1010 DE is depicted in below image.

An Intercompany purchase order 4500003515 with quantity 5pc of purchase order type NBIC (IC SiT STO) is created at receiving plant 1010 with EWM managed storage location EROD at purchasing organization 1010 DE receiving company 1010 DE with intercompany vendor BP1810 representing delivering company 1810 US.

An outbound delivery 80004929 of delivery type NCC3 is created at US delivering plant 1810 with storage location UAFS.

Goods issue is executed on the corresponding outbound delivery order (ODO) 80004931 at EWM warehouse U002 at delivering company US.

Goods issue on EWM ODO 80004931 triggered PGI (Post gods issue) on LE delivery 80004931 which subsequently triggered VSiT postings (with movement types 681, 685, 107) and created inbound delivery 180001751 as shown with app Monitor value chains.

 

The inbound delivery 180001751 is created automatically with delivery type ELST (IDel. Val. Rec. SiT) at receiving plant 1010 DE with storage location EROD .

The new feature of 2025 FPS01 allows receipt of different quantity with exception code like DIFW (Diff. as Charges for Warehouse) but not with DIFD (Diff. as Charges for Inbound Delivery) as DIFD updates delivery quantity. A warehouse task is created on the inbound delivery  180001751 and the error Quantity change is not allowed with valuated SIT in an STO process.Message No. /SCWM/DELIVERY758 appears when confirming 4 pc for delivery quantity 5 pc with exception code DIFD.

System accepts the confirmation of 4 pc with exception code DIFW.

Difference in quantity needs to be updated with difference analyzer /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER.

The pre-requisite configuration to achieve the functionality is to flag the new checkbox “Prevent quantity changes for stock transport order” for the inbound item type IDLV for document category PDI.

There isa new field at the item level of the inbound delivery indicating the STO type (E Advanced intercompany process with valuated stock in transit).

Purchase order history shows the relevant movement types for VSiT.

This blog is based on my personal tests , observations , insights in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. Will appreciate your feedback / comments.
